title: VimFu — Ctrl-L
description: Ctrl-L redraws the screen. Clears visual glitches without quitting Vim.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

steps.say: Control L redraws the screen. If your terminal ever looks garbled or glitchy, this fixes it without quitting Vim.
The screen looks fine now, but let's press control L anyway.
The entire screen refreshed. Everything re-rendered cleanly. You might not notice anything when the display is already correct.
You'll need this when a background process prints to your terminal and messes up Vim's display. Or when you resize your terminal window and things look off.
Some people also use colon redraw exclamation mark, which does the same thing from command mode.
Clean again. No need to quit and reopen.
Control L. Redraw the screen. Your panic button for display glitches.
